question: [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the comment 'The taker will gain at least minUnits' (line 178) does not document that minUnits == 0 is a valid input that disables the minimum units check, under preconditions: minUnits == 0, filledUnits == 0 (all takes revert); call sequence: call buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with minUnits = 0 and all-reverting takes; invariant tested: when minUnits == 0, the check 'require(filledUnits >= minUnits, UnitsTooLow())' always passes, meaning the taker may receive 0 units while paying targetBuyerAssets; scoped impact: documentation mismatch — the comment implies minUnits provides a meaningful protection, but minUnits == 0 disables it entirely; proof idea: unit test with minUnits = 0 and all-reverting takes, assert that the function reverts with OutOfOffers before the minUnits check.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the comment 'The taker will lose at most maxUnits' (line 250) does not document that maxUnits == type(uint256).max is a valid input that disables the maximum units check, under preconditions: maxUnits == type(uint256).max; call sequence: call supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget with maxUnits = type(uint256).max; invariant tested: the check 'require(filledUnits <= maxUnits, UnitsTooHigh())' always passes when maxUnits == type(uint256).max; scoped impact: documentation mismatch — the comment implies maxUnits provides a meaningful slippage protection, but type(uint256).max disables it entirely; proof idea: unit test with maxUnits = type(uint256).max, assert that the function does not document this as a valid way to disable the check.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the comment 'Assumes that id matches offer.market' (line 13) is not enforced in the bundler, and that passing an offer with a market that does not match id would cause consumableUnits to return a value based on the wrong market's consumed state, under preconditions: offer.market does not match id (e.g., due to a bug in the caller); call sequence: call consumableUnits with id = toId(marketA) but offer.market = marketB; invariant tested: the assumption is documented but not enforced; scoped impact: missing edge-case test coverage — the bundler's InconsistentMarket check on line 73 prevents this in practice, but the library itself has no guard; proof idea: unit test consumableUnits with mismatched id and offer.market, assert the return value is incorrect and document that the caller is responsible for the invariant.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the comment 'Unusable with tokens that revert on such a sequence: approve(..., 0); approve(..., type(uint256).max)' (line 24) does not document the behavior when the token reverts on approve(..., type(uint256).max) but not on approve(..., 0), under preconditions: loanToken reverts on approve(spender, type(uint256).max) but not on approve(spender, 0); call sequence: call any bundle function with such a loanToken; invariant tested: the comment only documents the combined revert case, not the partial revert case; scoped impact: documentation mismatch — the bundler may successfully call approve(0) but then revert on approve(max), leaving the allowance at 0 and causing the subsequent transfer to fail; proof idea: unit test with a mock token that reverts on approve(max) but not approve(0), assert the bundler reverts with ApproveReturnedFalse.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the comment 'The msg.sender should have approved the bundler to transfer enough collateral' (line 113) does not document the behavior when collateralSupplies[i].assets == 0, under preconditions: collateralSupplies[i].assets == 0; call sequence: call supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget with collateralSupplies[0].assets = 0; invariant tested: when assets == 0, pullToken pulls 0 tokens and supplyCollateral is called with 0 assets, which may be a no-op or may revert; scoped impact: missing edge-case test coverage — the comment does not document whether zero-asset collateral supply is valid; proof idea: unit test with collateralSupplies[0].assets = 0, assert the function either succeeds as a no-op or reverts with a descriptive error.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: Consume.spec takeConsumedBoundedByMax rule] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the Certora Consume.spec rule 'takeConsumedBoundedByMax' (line 59) only checks 'offer.maxAssets > 0 => consumed <= offer.maxAssets' and 'offer.maxAssets == 0 => consumed <= offer.maxUnits', but does not cover the case where both offer.maxAssets == 0 and offer.maxUnits == 0, under preconditions: offer.maxAssets == 0, offer.maxUnits == 0; call sequence: call take() with such an offer; invariant tested: the Certora rule has a gap for offers with both limits at zero; scoped impact: unsafe assumption in Certora specs — the consumed bound is not proved for the zero-zero case, meaning an offer with both limits at zero could theoretically be filled without bound; proof idea: check whether Midnight.sol enforces a bound on consumed when both maxAssets and maxUnits are zero, and add a Certora rule or unit test for this case.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the comment 'touchMarket to have the correct settlement fees' (line 63) does not document that touchMarket creates the market if it does not exist, potentially initializing it with default fee parameters that differ from what the taker expects, under preconditions: market does not exist yet; call sequence: call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with a market that has not been touched; invariant tested: the bundler may inadvertently create a market with default fees, which could differ from the fees the taker computed off-chain; scoped impact: documentation mismatch — the comment implies touchMarket only updates fees, but it also creates the market; proof idea: unit test with a non-existent market, assert that touchMarket creates it with default fees and that the taker's expected settlement fee matches.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the comment 'If taking an offer reverts, the bundler will completely skip this offer' (line 44) does not document that if the revert is due to a gas exhaustion (out-of-gas), the try/catch will not catch it and the entire bundle will revert, under preconditions: take() consumes all remaining gas; call sequence: call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with a gas limit just below what take() needs; invariant tested: the comment implies all reverts are caught, but out-of-gas is not catchable by try/catch in Solidity; scoped impact: documentation mismatch — the comment misleads integrators into thinking the bundler is robust to all take failures; proof idea: unit test with a gas-consuming take callback, assert that out-of-gas propagates through the try/catch.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the comment 'The referral fee changes the amount that must be filled, which can change the average taking price' (line 251) does not document that when referralFeePct is close to WAD-1, targetFilledSellerAssets = targetSellerAssets + referralFeeAssets may overflow uint256, under preconditions: referralFeePct = WAD - 1, targetSellerAssets = type(uint256).max / WAD; call sequence: call supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget with near-max targetSellerAssets and near-max referralFeePct; invariant tested: line 278 'uint256 targetFilledSellerAssets = targetSellerAssets + referralFeeAssets' may overflow; scoped impact: missing edge-case test coverage — Solidity 0.8.34 would revert on overflow, but the comment does not document this revert condition; proof idea: fuzz targetSellerAssets and referralFeePct near their maximum values, assert the addition on line 278 does not overflow.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: Solvency.spec pendingFeeReceiptZero invariant] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the Certora Solvency.spec 'pendingFeeReceiptZero' invariant (line 157) is declared as a 'weak invariant' meaning it only holds after each transaction, not before each external call, but the comment 'For any token, the pending settlement fee receipt after a transaction is 0' does not document the implication that during a transaction (e.g., inside a callback), pendingFeeReceipt may be non-zero, under preconditions: maker callback is called during take(); call sequence: call take() with a maker callback that reads claimableSettlementFee; invariant tested: the weak invariant allows pendingFeeReceipt to be non-zero during callbacks, meaning claimableSettlementFee may appear inflated during a callback; scoped impact: unsafe assumption in Certora specs — integrators relying on claimableSettlementFee during callbacks may observe incorrect values; proof idea: unit test with a maker callback that reads claimableSettlementFee, assert the value matches the expected post-transaction value.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the comment 'The taker must have authorized this bundler and the msg.sender (if different from the taker) on Midnight' (line 39) does not document that the authorization check on line 60 uses isAuthorized(taker, msg.sender) which is coarse-grained (not scoped by action or market), meaning an authorized msg.sender can call any bundle function on behalf of the taker, under preconditions: taker has authorized msg.sender for a different purpose; call sequence: authorized msg.sender calls buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral on behalf of taker without taker's intent; invariant tested: the comment implies authorization is specific to this bundler, but Midnight's authorization is global; scoped impact: documentation mismatch — the comment does not warn that authorizing any address on Midnight grants full control over all bundle operations; proof idea: unit test where taker authorizes msg.sender for repay only, assert that msg.sender can also call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral on behalf of taker.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the comment 'Reverts if buyerPrice > WAD, because not all buyerAssets are reachable then' (line 14) does not document that this revert propagates through ConsumableUnitsLib and is not caught by the bundler's try/catch, causing the entire bundle to revert rather than skipping the offer, under preconditions: offer.tick corresponds to a buyerPrice > WAD; call sequence: call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with such an offer; invariant tested: the bundler comment says 'Reverts if ConsumableUnitsLib reverts' (line 43), which is consistent, but the TakeAmountsLib comment does not cross-reference this propagation behavior; scoped impact: documentation mismatch — the TakeAmountsLib comment implies the revert is a local check, but it propagates to the bundler level; proof idea: unit test with offer.tick = MAX_TICK and offer.buy = false, assert the bundle reverts with PriceGreaterThanOne.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the comment 'The receiver will receive at least minSellerAssets' (line 114) does not document that minSellerAssets == 0 disables the minimum check, and that when referralFeePct == 0 and minSellerAssets == 0, the receiver may receive 0 assets if all takes revert, under preconditions: minSellerAssets == 0, referralFeePct == 0, all takes revert; call sequence: call supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget with minSellerAssets = 0 and all-reverting takes; invariant tested: the OutOfOffers check on line 163 fires before the minSellerAssets check on line 166, so the receiver protection is irrelevant when all takes revert; scoped impact: documentation mismatch — the comment implies minSellerAssets is the primary protection, but OutOfOffers is the actual guard; proof idea: unit test with minSellerAssets = 0 and all-reverting takes, assert the function reverts with OutOfOffers.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral, buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker observe that neither function documents the behavior when collateralWithdrawals is empty but collateralReceiver is address(0), under preconditions: collateralWithdrawals.length == 0, collateralReceiver == address(0); call sequence: call either function with empty collateralWithdrawals and collateralReceiver = address(0); invariant tested: when collateralWithdrawals is empty, collateralReceiver is never used, so address(0) is silently accepted; scoped impact: missing edge-case test coverage — the comment does not document that collateralReceiver is ignored when collateralWithdrawals is empty, which may confuse integrators; proof idea: unit test with empty collateralWithdrawals and collateralReceiver = address(0), assert the function succeeds without sending tokens to address(0).

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the comment 'This function pulls maxBuyerAssets from the msg.sender and transfers back the remaining tokens at the end' (line 45) does not document the behavior when the loanToken is a rebasing token that increases the bundler's balance between the pull and the refund, under preconditions: loanToken is a rebasing token that increases balances; call sequence: call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with a rebasing loanToken, observe that the bundler's balance increases during execution; invariant tested: the refund on line 104 sends exactly maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets, not the actual bundler balance; scoped impact: missing edge-case test coverage — rebasing gains are permanently locked in the bundler; proof idea: unit test with a rebasing loanToken, assert that the bundler's loanToken balance is zero after the function.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: Midnight.spec noCreditAndDebt invariant] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the Certora Midnight.spec 'noCreditAndDebt' invariant (line 160) states 'A user cannot have both credit and debt' but MidnightBundles.sol's take loop may create a state where the taker has both credit and debt transiently during a multi-offer fill, under preconditions: taker has existing debt, first take reduces debt to zero and creates credit, second take creates new debt; call sequence: call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with two offers where the first reduces taker's debt and the second increases it; invariant tested: the noCreditAndDebt invariant must hold after each take, not just after the bundle; scoped impact: unsafe assumption in Certora specs — the invariant is proved per-take, but the bundler's multi-take loop is not covered; proof idea: unit test with a taker who has debt, fill two offers that first reduce then increase debt, assert noCreditAndDebt holds after each take.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged repayer observe that the comment 'Fee = assets * pct / WAD; units repaid = assets - fee' (line 313) does not document that when referralFeePct == 0, units == assets, meaning the repayer must pass the exact debt amount in assets, under preconditions: referralFeePct == 0, assets == debt; call sequence: call repayAndWithdrawCollateral with referralFeePct = 0 and assets = exact debt; invariant tested: when referralFeePct == 0, the formula simplifies to units = assets, which is consistent with the comment; scoped impact: missing edge-case test coverage — the comment does not document the zero-fee case explicitly, which may confuse integrators who expect the formula to always require a gross-up; proof idea: unit test with referralFeePct = 0, assert units == assets and the debt is fully repaid.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Low Invariant documentation mismatch, misleading comments, unsafe assumptions in Certora specs, or missing edge-case test coverage'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the comment 'Reverts if ConsumableUnitsLib reverts' (line 43) does not document that ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits calls IMidnight(midnight).consumed(offer.maker, offer.group) which is a view call that could revert if the market is not created, under preconditions: market is not created (tickSpacing == 0); call sequence: call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral before touchMarket creates the market; invariant tested: touchMarket is called on line 64 before the take loop, so the market should be created; but if touchMarket fails silently, consumed() may revert; scoped impact: missing edge-case test coverage — the ordering of touchMarket and consumableUnits is not documented as a dependency; proof idea: unit test where touchMarket is called with an invalid market, assert that consumableUnits does not revert unexpectedly.
